prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: Warhammer Fantasy / The Old World inspired grimdark world map for the terrain type continent_archipelago.
Scene/backdrop: one dominant grim old-world continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago, empire cities and river towns, dark Chaos-tainted forests, northern wastes, storm-wracked seas, dwarf holds in mountain chains, blighted marshes, ruined citadels, plague islands, pirate coves, witch-hunter routes and corrupted island shrines.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated dark fantasy atlas, old world cartography, aged parchment, inked terrain relief, oppressive storm atmosphere, grim battle-worn campaign map with corruption scars and sinister wilderness markings.
Composition/framing: tall portrait map; main continent off-center with an irregular archipelago sweeping along the coast in a diagonal arc; islands include tiny islets, reef belts, crescent chains, large fortress islands, plague isles and jagged volcanic rocks; include compass rose, severe border, sea routes, fortress icons and regional boundaries without readable text.
Geography constraints: islands must be irregularly distributed and varied in size and shape, with channels, reefs, wreck fields, atolls, volcanic outcrops and broken shores; continental coastline should include peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, curved coasts, deltas, fjords, capes, marshy estuaries and fragmented cliffs; show frozen wastes, dark forests, empire plains, swamps, mountains, moors and chaos-blasted badlands.
Avoid: uniform island grid, simple round islands, cheerful fantasy colors, modern satellite image, board-game UI, readable labels, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
